## User Engagement Teardown: Pinterest

**Published by**

September 21, 2015

![](https://iterable.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/092115_User-Engagement-Teardown-Pinterest.png)

In this [User Engagement Teardown](https://iterable.com/teardown), we are taking a look at [Pinterest](https://pinterest.com), the wildly successful visual bookmarking or “pinning” site. As of October 2016, they have over [150M active users](https://blog.pinterest.com/en/150-million-people-finding-ideas-pinterest)!

You would expect Pinterest to have done a good job of user engagement and that is indeed what we found. We created an account on Pinterest, studied how they communicated with their subscribers across the channels in a course of three weeks.

Take a look at the slides below for more — we think there are some great lessons and ideas there for all marketers who want to drive their engagement with users.

_(Note: if you can’t see the embedded SlideShare, make sure to turn off any ad blockers!)_

[https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/embed\_code/key/358rLsp8a2fqBy](https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/embed_code/key/358rLsp8a2fqBy)

_Note: Pinterest is not an Iterable customer._
